steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage ends by naming the core problem: inconsistent definitions of urban settlements across countries make it difficult to compare urbanization research. The correct answer must capture that comparison problem.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It is difficult for researchers to compare aspects of urbanization globally because of the various ways different countries define urban settlements.: Matches the passage's final sentence and the argument it builds throughout. ✓
  • Choice B — Even though countries use different methods to define urban settlements, researchers consider any area having a population of at least 10,000 to be urban.: Contradicts or distorts what the passage actually says the definitions are. ✗
  • Choice C — Researchers focus on factors other than population when conducting studies on urbanization in Greenland.: Introduces information not present in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — Urbanization is easily compared between countries when they have similar total populations.: States something the passage directly argues against. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
